description: Financial configuration for Cricket Manager - revenues, expenses, and prize money

initialBudgets.description: $10M USD per team at season start (separate from auction budget)

prizeMoneyPerWin.description: $50K bonus for each match victory during league stage

seasonEndPrizes.description: Prize money distributed based on final league standings

revenueStreams.description: Revenue sources for teams - basic formulas that scale with performance

sponsorship.description: Annual sponsorship deals based on previous season standing (or initial for first season)

matchTickets.description: Ticket revenue per home match - increases with better performance

performanceBonus.description: Base $20K per home match. +$2K per win, -$500 per loss in last 5 matches

broadcastRights.description: Broadcast revenue share - increases with viewership (tied to performance)

performanceMultiplier.description: Base $30K per match. Top 4 teams get 1.5x, top 7 get 1.2x, bottom 3 get 0.8x

listingFee.description: No fee to list players for sale (may add 1-2% in future)

transferTax.description: No transfer taxes in current implementation (may add 5-10% in future)

minimumBidIncrement.description: Bids must be at least 5% higher than current offer, minimum $10K increment

minimumReserve.description: Teams should maintain at least $500K reserve (warning only, not enforced)

budgetLimits.description_negative: Teams cannot spend beyond their available budget

revenueCalculationTiming.description: When revenues are calculated and distributed

notes: Revenue formulas are basic placeholders - can be enhanced with more complex calculations
Performance-based revenues use simple multipliers for MVP phase
No recurring wage costs - player acquisition is one-time cost
Transfer system uses this config for budget validation and fee calculations
